title: A walk I take when I need to think
summary: Describe a route you walk when you want to clear your mind, and explain how it makes you feel.
topic: A walk I take when I need to think
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a walk you take when you need to think or feel better. Describe the route step by step and say what you see or hear along the way. Use present simple for habits and comparatives to describe how you feel before and after.
hintStarters: When I need to clear my head, I always walk to…
The path starts at… and ends at…
After the walk, I always feel…

hintVocab.meaningVi: xua tan suy nghĩ, làm đầu óc thoáng hơn

sampleText: When I need to clear my head, I always walk along the canal near my neighbourhood in Amsterdam. I start at the bridge on my street and walk east. The path is narrow and made of cobblestones, so I have to watch my feet a little. On one side there is the water, dark green and slow. On the other side there are tall brick houses with long windows. I pass by a small coffee stand where an old man sells takeaway cups every morning. I never stop, but I always smell the coffee as I walk past. After about fifteen minutes, the path opens onto a small park with a few benches and some big trees. I usually sit on the bench near the water for five minutes. I do not do anything — I just look at the ducks and the boats. Then I walk back the same way. The whole walk takes about thirty minutes. Before the walk, I feel tight in my chest and my thoughts are faster than usual. After the walk, I feel calmer and my problems seem smaller than they did before. I think it is not about the distance — it is about moving your body and looking at something that is not a screen.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: hẹp và được lát bằng đá cuội

sampleAnnotations.phrase: tall brick houses with long windows
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: những ngôi nhà gạch cao với cửa sổ dài

sampleAnnotations.phrase: smell the coffee as I walk past
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: ngửi mùi cà phê khi tôi đi ngang qua
mở ra một công viên nhỏ

sampleAnnotations.phrase: my thoughts are faster than usual
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: suy nghĩ của tôi nhanh hơn bình thường

sampleAnnotations.phrase: seem smaller than they did before
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: có vẻ nhỏ hơn so với trước

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Comparatives for before / after contrast
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng tính từ so sánh hơn để đối chiếu cảm xúc trước và sau khi đi bộ (feel calmer, seem smaller, faster than usual). Thêm '-er' vào tính từ ngắn hoặc dùng 'more' với tính từ dài.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use comparative adjectives to contrast how you feel before and after ('calmer', 'smaller', 'faster than usual'). Add '-er' to short adjectives or use 'more' with longer ones.

sampleTranslationVi: Khi cần xua tan suy nghĩ, tôi luôn đi bộ dọc theo kênh đào gần khu phố của tôi ở Amsterdam. Tôi bắt đầu từ cây cầu trên đường của mình và đi về phía đông. Con đường hẹp và được lát bằng đá cuội, nên tôi phải hơi chú ý vào chân. Một bên là mặt nước, xanh thẫm và chảy chậm. Phía bên kia là những ngôi nhà gạch cao với cửa sổ dài. Tôi đi qua một quán cà phê nhỏ nơi một ông lão bán cốc mang đi mỗi buổi sáng. Tôi không bao giờ dừng lại, nhưng lúc nào cũng ngửi mùi cà phê khi đi ngang qua. Sau khoảng mười lăm phút, con đường mở ra một công viên nhỏ với vài ghế băng và những cây to. Tôi thường ngồi trên chiếc ghế gần mặt nước khoảng năm phút. Tôi không làm gì — chỉ nhìn những con vịt và những chiếc thuyền. Rồi tôi đi về theo cùng con đường. Cả chuyến đi bộ mất khoảng ba mươi phút. Trước khi đi, tôi cảm thấy tức ngực và suy nghĩ nhanh hơn bình thường. Sau khi đi, tôi cảm thấy bình tĩnh hơn và các vấn đề có vẻ nhỏ hơn so với trước. Tôi nghĩ không phải về khoảng cách — mà là về việc di chuyển cơ thể và nhìn vào thứ gì đó không phải màn hình.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which phrase is a natural idiom for relaxing or emptying your mind?
options: clear my head
clean my head
empty my head
open my head
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Clear my head' is the natural idiom for removing stress or busy thoughts.
mcQuestions.hint: Which is the natural phrasal verb for clearing your thoughts?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct comparative: 'After the walk, I feel ___ than before.'
options: calmer
more calm
calmest
calmly
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Calmer' is the comparative form of the short adjective 'calm'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct present simple sentence describing a walking route.
options: I start at the bridge on my street and walk east.
I started at the bridge on my street and walked east.
I am starting at the bridge on my street and walking east.
I was starting at the bridge on my street and walking east.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present simple describes a habit or regular action.
mcQuestions.hint: A habitual walk is described in present simple.

options: I pass by a small coffee stand.
I pass from a small coffee stand.
I pass through a small coffee stand.
I pass across a small coffee stand.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Pass by' means to go past something without stopping.
mcQuestions.hint: Which phrasal verb means to go past something without stopping?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct comparatives: 'My problems seem ___ than they did before.'
options: smaller
smallest
more small
smallness
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Smaller' is the comparative of the short adjective 'small'.
mcQuestions.hint: Add '-er' to one-syllable adjectives to form comparatives.

options: My thoughts are faster than usual.
My thoughts are faster than usually.
My thoughts are more fast than usual.
My thoughts are fastest than usual.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Faster than usual' is the correct form: comparative adjective + 'than usual'.
mcQuestions.hint: Use the comparative form of the adjective before 'than usual'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which phrase means the path leads to an open area?
options: the path opens onto a small park
the path opens into a small park
the path opens up a small park
the path opens for a small park
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Open onto' means a path or space leads directly to another space.
mcQuestions.hint: Which preposition follows 'opens' when describing a path leading to a place?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best describes a feeling of tension in the body?
options: I feel tight in my chest.
I feel tightly in my chest.
I feel tightness my chest.
I feel a tight of my chest.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Feel tight in + body part' is a natural way to describe physical tension.
mcQuestions.hint: Use an adjective after 'feel' and the preposition 'in' before the body part.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the best way to describe cobblestone streets.
options: The path is narrow and made of cobblestones.
The path is narrow and done by cobblestones.
The path is narrow and built with cobblestones.
The path is narrow and composed from cobblestones.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Made of' is the standard phrase for describing what material something is built from.
mcQuestions.hint: Which phrase naturally describes what a path is built from?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which word fills the gap? 'I have to watch my feet a ___.'
options: little
bit of
small
less
mcQuestions.explanation: 'A little' is used as an adverb to mean 'slightly' or 'somewhat'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best explains why the walk helps without using formal phrases?
options: I think it is not about the distance — it is about moving your body and looking at something that is not a screen.
In conclusion, walking is beneficial for mental health and overall well-being.
To sum up, taking a walk is always a good idea when you are stressed.
Therefore, I recommend walking as a solution to all problems.
mcQuestions.explanation: The first option uses personal, natural language without formulaic writing phrases.
mcQuestions.hint: Avoid 'In conclusion' and 'Therefore' in personal writing — they sound too formal.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Do you think walking helps people think more clearly? Why might moving the body help the mind? Have you ever solved a problem while walking?
